title: ferulago sylvatica subsp. confusa
intro: Ferulago sylvatica subsp. confusa, also known as the confused ferulago or wood fern, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carrot family Apiaceae. It is native to parts of Europe and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of various gastrointestinal issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Ferulago sylvatica subsp. confusa include relief from indigestion, bloating, and other symptoms associated with irritable bowel syndrome, as well as its potential anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of Ferulago sylvatica subsp. confusa typically include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
